content.full_text:
Office of the City Attorney
City of Pacifica
170 Santa Maria Avenue
Pacifica, California 94044

March 5, 1991

Fair Political Practices Commission
Legal Division
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Re: Request for Formal Written Advice

Dear Sir or Madam:

This letter constitutes a request for formal written advice pursuant to Government Code Section 83114. The request is submitted on behalf of Councilmember Bonnie Wells, a member of the City Council of the City of Pacifica. I have received specific authorization from Councilmember Wells to request formal written advice on her behalf.

QUESTION

As an employee of Pacific Gas and Electric Company, may Councilmember Wells vote to approve payment of the City's monthly PG&E utility bills?

MATERIAL FACTS

The facts material to the questions presented are as follows:

Councilmember Wells is employed as an Area Manager by the public utility, Pacific Gas and Electric (PG&E). PG&E is a source of income to Councilmember Wells of $250 or more in the preceding twelve months. PG&E is listed on the New York Stock Exchange. As an Area Manager, Ms. Wells is responsible for government and public relations. Her governmental duties do not include the City of Pacifica. She deals with consumer issues, relations with community groups, chambers of commerce and service organizations. She is not paid to influence, advocate or achieve any goals with respect to the City of Pacifica.

The City is a customer of PG&E. PG&E submits monthly utility bills to the City which are generally in the range of $70,000 per month. PG&E's monthly utility bills to the City are approved for payment by the City Council once a month. Is PG&E's interest directly involved in this decision such that Councilmember Wells is disqualified from voting to approve payment of the City's monthly PG&E bills? (See for example

Fair Political Practices Commission  
March 5, 1991  
Page 2

your recent advice letter I-90-518 (Thomas)). We would appreciate your formal written advice concerning this issue.

I realize there may be potential Government Code Section 1090 questions involved in this request which are not within your jurisdiction.

Lastly, I understand there may be existing advice letters concerning potential conflicts of employees of public utilities such as PG&E and Pacific Bell who serve as public officials. I would appreciate receiving copies of any such advice letters. These letters may address potential conflict issues likely to face Councilmember Wells in the future, since public utility employees often face similar conflict questions.

Thank you for your cooperation.

Very truly yours,

[Susan Schectman's signature]
SUSAN M. SCHECTMAN
City Attorney

SMS:fd

cc: Bonnie Wells
